stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 5, 2026: revenue Rs 4,450.4 cr (+30% YoY); net profit Rs 612.3 cr (+134% YoY) aided by ~Rs 181 cr deferred-tax-liability reversal from Cigniti merger. FY26 dollar revenue growth 29.2%, EBIT margin +370 bps to 14.4%. Cigniti amalgamation effective May 5, 2026 (1:1 swap, record date May 16). FY27 EBITDA guided >20.5% on USD 1.75B order executable. Q1 FY27 explicitly guided FLAT QoQ on India-business exit; growth resumes Q2 FY27. Board DEFERRED dividend. Stock surged ~10% on May 6 to intraday high Rs 1,294 on >6.8x volume.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT is the worst-performing sectoral index in 2026 - down ~17-25% YTD vs Nifty -10-12% - on GenAI-billing-deflation fears, weak EU/US discretionary spend, and soft FY27 guidance from tier-1 peers (TCS/Infosys/HCLT). Mid-cap IT (Coforge, Persistent) is outpacing large-cap on revenue growth. Coforge dipped up to 11% intra-month with the broader IT sell-off in late April before the post-results rally.
stocks.peer_comparison: Coforge's 29.2% FY26 dollar revenue growth materially outpaces tier-1 peers (low-single-digit growth). EBIT margin expansion of 370 bps and order book of USD 1.75B compare favorably. Stock still down ~35% from 52-week high of Rs 1,994 - sharpest drawdown in mid-cap IT cohort. Trades at a discount to Persistent Systems despite similar growth profile.
red_flags: Dividend DEFERRED at the May 5 board meeting - atypical post a strong Q4 print
Q1 FY27 revenue guided FLAT QoQ on India-business exit - near-term growth-optics overhang
Q4 PAT optics flattered by one-time deferred-tax-liability reversal of ~Rs 181 cr
EVP & Head of M&A Anup Kumar and Head of Investor Relations Manish Hemrajani both resigned effective April 18, 2026 - senior departures during integration
10% pop on May 6 may already price in the positives heading into the flat Q1 print
catalysts: Cigniti merger record date May 16, 2026 (share allotment to Cigniti shareholders)
Q2 FY27 growth resumption per guidance
FY27 EBITDA-margin trajectory toward >20.5%
Possible later dividend declaration once merger settles

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results scheduled May 30, 2026. Two recent USFDA approvals: Dextromethorphan Polistirex Oral Suspension (US market USD 138M to Feb 2026, launch slated Q2 FY27) and Glycerol Phenylbutyrate Oral Liquid. The Zentiva acquisition overhang has RESOLVED - Chicago PE firm GTCR won the bid at EUR 4.1B (~USD 4.8B), so Aurobindo is NOT taking on the previously-discussed USD 4.75B bridge loan. Open interest jumped ~20.6% to ~62K contracts, indicating bullish derivatives positioning. Stock at Rs 1,385.40 on May 5 - near 52-week high of Rs 1,459.50; +20.6% over last 6 months and +11.6% YoY.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Pharma is acting as a defensive in 2026 - down only ~3% YTD vs Nifty -10-12%. Tailwinds: rupee depreciation, double-digit India IPM growth (~12.4% YoY in Feb 2026), patent-expiry-driven complex generics. Headwind: Trump-administration US drug-import tariff overhang tilts gains toward domestic-formulations names; Aurobindo is heavily US-exposed.
stocks.peer_comparison: Aurobindo holds the largest ANDA pipeline among Indian pharma; trades at a valuation discount vs Sun Pharma due to higher US-generics exposure. Has lagged the Nifty Pharma rally led by domestic-tilted names like Dr Reddy's, Cipla. Recent USFDA approval cadence and OI build are stronger than most peers; relative price action (near 52WH) is better than Sun and DRL.
red_flags: Heavy US-revenue mix means elevated exposure to potential US drug-pricing or tariff action
Trading near 52-week high reduces margin-of-safety and increases mean-reversion risk into the May 30 print
Less defensive than domestic-focused pharma peers in a tariff scenario
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results scheduled May 30, 2026 with FY27 guidance
Q2 FY27 launch of Dextromethorphan Polistirex Oral Suspension (USD 138M addressable)
Continuing ANDA approval cadence
Removal of Zentiva-leverage overhang (GTCR won) is a positive recalibration vs prior consensus fear

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released May 6 (today): Q4 revenue +7% YoY. Board APPROVED Rs 2,300 cr next-gen refrigerants capex; DEFERRED Rs 490 cr BOPP packaging-films expansion. 9M FY26 prints were strong - revenue +8% to Rs 11,171 cr, PAT +73% to Rs 1,253 cr - led by chemicals momentum. Q3 PAT was Rs 432.66 cr (+59.6% YoY). Investor con-call May 6 at 11:30 IST. Stock up ~4% on May 6 to Rs 2,624; market cap ~Rs 75,630 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty-chemicals retains a secular growth narrative supported by HFC refrigerant pricing, China+1 export wins, and recovering domestic demand. SRF is the sole Indian manufacturer of F-134a/F-32 and the largest refrigerant producer; the August 2025 Chemours strategic agreements provide fluoropolymer/fluoroelastomer access (semicon/auto/aerospace). Specialty-chem segment compounded ~70% over 4 years. Packaging films remain weak industry-wide.
stocks.peer_comparison: Differentiated leader in fluorochemicals/refrigerants vs peers Navin Fluorine, Deepak Nitrite, PI Industries, Gujarat Fluorochem, Aarti Industries. Capex confidence (Rs 2,300 cr) signals stronger demand visibility than peers. Q3 PAT growth of +59.6% YoY at the top end of the cohort, but Q4 top-line growth (+7%) trails more aggressive specialty-chem peers.
red_flags: BOPP packaging-films capex (Rs 490 cr) DEFERRED - confirms weak packaging-films demand
Modest 7% Q4 top-line growth - sector peers growing faster
Stock has rallied for multiple quarters; valuation rich heading into the print
Concentrated Rs 2,300 cr refrigerants capex commitment increases execution risk
catalysts: Q4 FY26 investor con-call today (May 6) at 11:30 IST - capex-reallocation commentary key
Rs 2,300 cr next-gen refrigerants project execution timeline
Final FY26 dividend recommendation pending
Refrigerant-pricing cycle and Chemours collaboration ramp
Street EPS-growth forecast of ~19.6% per annum

stocks.recent_news: USFDA inspected Paithan, Maharashtra manufacturing facility April 13-21, 2026 and issued Form-483 with 5 observations on April 22 - company has committed to respond within stipulated timeline. Q4 FY26 earnings call held May 5 at 16:00 IST (results just out at session start). Stock at Rs 2,900 on May 5, +8.13% over last month. 52-week range Rs 2,329.90 - Rs 3,228; market cap ~Rs 36,240 cr. Q1 FY26 had shown +14% revenue growth driven by US generics.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Pharma defensive (-3% YTD vs Nifty -10-12%) with rupee tailwind, but US-tariff risk overhang continues. Domestic-focused names favored over US-generics-heavy names. Ajanta has a balanced mix (India branded generics + emerging markets + US generics) which partially insulates it.
stocks.peer_comparison: Ajanta has historically run higher margins than US-generics-heavy peers (Aurobindo, Lupin) due to branded-formulations exposure. Recent monthly outperformance (+8.13%) is roughly in line with the mid-cap pharma cohort. Form-483 with 5 observations is a peer-group-specific overhang vs pharma names without recent USFDA actions.
red_flags: USFDA Form-483 with 5 observations at Paithan (April 22, 2026) - not a warning letter but a regulatory overhang until response is accepted (EIR vs OAI classification pending)
Q4 print reception unclear - earnings call concluded after market hours May 5; no clean post-print reaction visible yet
Trading in upper half of 52-week range with limited margin-of-safety
Specifics of the 5 observations were not publicly disclosed
catalysts: Market reaction to Q4 FY26 results announced May 5 (post-call digestion on May 6)
USFDA response acceptance and inspection-classification (EIR vs OAI) on Paithan
New product launches in US generics segment
Mid-to-high single-digit growth guidance per management commentary

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 5: consolidated PAT +42% YoY to Rs 4,668 cr on revenue +29% to Rs 54,982 cr. FY26 PAT +32% to Rs 17,099 cr (highest ever); FY26 revenue +25% to ~Rs 1.98 lakh cr. Final dividend Rs 33/share recommended (+30% YoY). AGM scheduled July 30, 2026; dividend record date July 3. Auto held #1 SUV position with >25% revenue market share - 11.17 lakh vehicles in FY26 (+19%). Tractor volumes crossed 5 lakh in FY26 - first ever - with market share >43%. Stock +1.72% on May 6 to Rs 3,265.90.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Auto near record highs (~29,179 in early Jan 2026). April 2026 auto-retail at 26.11 lakh units (+12.94% YoY, highest-ever April). Tractors fastest-growing segment (+23.22% YoY in April) on strong rural sentiment and favorable farm economics. SUV demand robust. FADA dealer outlook for May positive on wedding-season demand and new-FY schemes.
stocks.peer_comparison: M&M is a clear sector leader: SUV market-share leadership (>25% revenue share), tractor market-share at multi-year high (>43%), and growth rates well above sector averages. Tata Motors and Maruti have faced more cyclical pressure. M&M's diversified auto + farm + financial-services + group-companies exposure is a relative strength versus peers.
red_flags: Stock has rallied substantially into and after the print - relative valuation tightening
EV strategy execution remains a multi-quarter watch item flagged in pre-results sell-side notes
Some commodity/cost-pressure risk noted by analysts heading into Q4
catalysts: Final dividend record date July 3, 2026; AGM July 30, 2026
Monthly auto retail/wholesale prints (May 2026 data ahead)
Wedding-season and pre-monsoon rural demand into May-June
Upcoming SUV/EV model launches in focus per sell-side
